channel.title: Texts on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in Texts on emsenn.net

item.title: The Catuṣkoṭi and Western Logic
item.description:
The relationship between the catuṣkoṭi and Western logical frameworks is contested, and the contestation reveals as much about the assumptions of Western logic as about the catuṣkoṭi itself.

Many-valued logic reading. The catuṣkoṭi can be modeled as a four-valued logic with truth values {T, F, B, N} (true, false, both, neither), paralleling Belnap’s four-valued logic for reasoning with incomplete and inconsistent information. This reading captures the structure — four exhaustive, mutually exclusive positions — but misses the therapeutic function. In Belnap’s system, the four values describe an agent’s epistemic state (what they’ve been told); in Nāgārjuna’s deployment, the four corners are exhausted and rejected, not occupied.
